# anyscale_cloud (Data Source)

Use this data source to retrieve information about an existing Anyscale Cloud. You can look up a cloud by its ID or name.

## Example Usage

### Look up by ID

```terraform
data "anyscale_cloud" "example" {
  id = "cld_abc123xyz"
}

# Use the cloud in a compute config
resource "anyscale_compute_config" "example" {
  name     = "my-compute-config"
  cloud_id = data.anyscale_cloud.example.id

  head_node = {
    instance_type = "m5.large"
  }
}
```

### Look up by Name

```terraform
data "anyscale_cloud" "production" {
  name = "production-cloud"
}

output "cloud_provider" {
  value = data.anyscale_cloud.production.cloud_provider
}

output "cloud_region" {
  value = data.anyscale_cloud.production.region
}
```

### Reference Existing Cloud for Multiple Resources

```terraform
data "anyscale_cloud" "shared" {
  name = "shared-dev-cloud"
}

resource "anyscale_compute_config" "team_a" {
  name     = "team-a-config"
  cloud_id = data.anyscale_cloud.shared.id

  head_node = {
    instance_type = "m5.large"
  }
}

resource "anyscale_compute_config" "team_b" {
  name     = "team-b-config"
  cloud_id = data.anyscale_cloud.shared.id

  head_node = {
    instance_type = "m5.xlarge"
  }
}
```

## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the cloud. Either `id` or `name` must be specified.
- `name` (String) The name of the cloud. Either `id` or `name` must be specified. If multiple clouds have the same name, the most recently created one will be returned.

### Read-Only

- `cloud_provider` (String) The cloud provider (AWS, GCP, AZURE, or GENERIC).
- `region` (String) The region where the cloud is deployed.
- `status` (String) The operational status of the cloud (e.g., ready, pending, failed).
- `state` (String) The lifecycle state of the cloud (e.g., ACTIVE, CREATING, FAILED).
- `is_empty_cloud` (Boolean) Whether this is an empty cloud (created without embedded resource configuration).
- `cloud_deployment_id` (String) The cloud deployment ID. For K8S clouds, this is passed to the Anyscale operator during installation.
- `auto_add_user` (Boolean) Whether users are automatically added to this cloud.
- `enable_lineage_tracking` (Boolean) Whether lineage tracking is enabled for this cloud.
- `enable_log_ingestion` (Boolean) Whether aggregated log ingestion is enabled for this cloud.

## Notes

- When looking up by name, if multiple clouds exist with the same name, the most recently created cloud will be returned, and a warning will be logged.
- The data source performs a read-only query and does not modify any infrastructure.
- Ensure you have appropriate permissions in your Anyscale account to list and read cloud configurations.
